titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/53

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=335 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:26:29.01456+00 | translation moa=336 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:26:30.846821+00

div.p:
.W. Rex scottorum . Omnibus probis hominibus terre sue . Clericis et laicis . francis . Anglis . et Scottis . salutem . Sciant tam posteri quam presentes
me concessisse et hac mea carta confirmasse Ecclesie Sancte Marie de Maylros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus donationem
quam Gospatricus comes fecit eidem ecclesie et monachis consensu et concessu heredis sui de terra hertesheued et spot et de quadam salina
pro salute anime sue et animarum predecessorum suorum . in omnibus ita libere et quiete in elemosina perpetua  sicut liberius et quietius carta ipsius Gospa
trici eis testatur  et carta regis Malcolmi fratris mei concedit et confirmat . Teste Ricardo episcopo Sancti Andree . Engelramo Episcopo
Glasguensi . Iohanne abbas de kelchou . Osberto abbate de Gedewrde . Nicholao cancellario . Matheo Archidiacono .S. Andree
Ricardo. De Morauilla . Waltero filio alani . Waltero Corbet . Roberto Auenel . Gilleberto filio Richerii . Philippo de Valunnes
Bernard[o] fili[o] [written Bernardꝰo filiꝰ] Breani . Roberto filio Guidonis . Radulfo de Cler . Willelmo de Mortemer . Ricardo Capellano . Imero cleri
co . Willelmo de haia . Radulfo namo . Waltero de berchelai . Apud Lannarc .

William by the grace of God king of the Scots sends greeting to all worthy men of his land, French, English and Scots. Let both those to come as well as those present know me to have granted and by this my charter to have made firm to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the donation that Earl Cospatric has made to the same church and monks by the agreement and permission of his heir in respect of the land Hartside and Spot, and of a saltpan, for the salvation of his soul and the souls of his predecessors, in all things so freely and quietly in everlasting alms, as freely and quietly the charter of Cospatric himself for them bears witness. Witness Richard bishop of St Andrews, Ingram bishop of Glasgow, John abbot of Kelso, Osbert abbot of Jedburgh, Nicholas the chancellor, Matthew the archdeacon of St Andrews, Richard de Morville, Walter son of Alan, Walter Corbet, Robert Avenel, Gilbert son of Richer, Philip de Volognes, Bernard son of Brian, Robert son of Guy, Ralph de Clare, William de Mortimer, Richard the chaplain, Imer the clerk, William de la Haye, Ralph le Nain, Walter of Barclay. At Lanark.
